recent_thoughts.thought: I'm fascinated by the complexity of cytochrome P450 enzymes and their diverse roles in various organisms. The fact that there are over 50,000 known variants of CYP enzymes is mind-boggling. I'm also intrigued by the specific examples given, such as their involvement in the metabolism of drugs, toxins, and even the breakdown of environmental pollutants. The article mentions that CYP enzymes are responsible for detoxifying 75% of the substances that enter the body, which is an impressive feat. I'm curious to learn more about how these enzymes work and their specific mechanisms.

recent_thoughts.thought: I'm intrigued by the concept of Area Under the Curve (AUC) and its application in pharmacokinetics. It seems to be a way to quantify the exposure of a substance in the body over time. I'm also interested in how AUC can be used to estimate the maximum concentration of a substance in the body and its clearance rate. The idea of using AUC to evaluate the pharmacokinetic properties of a substance is fascinating. I'm not entirely clear on how AUC is calculated, but it seems to involve integrating the concentration of the substance over time. I'd like to learn more about the mathematical aspects of AUC.
